forecast.title: Tomorrow's Outlook
forecast.explanation: We analyze the relationship between your recent recovery and accumulated fatigue to predict your optimal performance window.
forecast.whyReadiness: Your 3-day averages exceed your 7-day ones, indicating rising capacity.
forecast.whyVolume: You've accumulated significant volume; the algorithm prioritizes muscle recovery despite good scores.
forecast.whyStandard: Your metrics show physiological stability, suggesting standard capacity.

calculation.needOura: We need at least 3 days of Oura data to generate your forecast.
calculation.moreData: We need more consistency in recent data.

peak.title: Peak Performance

good.title: Good Forecast

caution.title: Caution

recovery.title: Recovery Needed

sleep_focus.title: Prioritize Sleep
sleep_focus.copy: 😴 Low sleep today. Go to bed early to optimize tomorrow.

steady.title: Steady

feedback.greatWeek: Great week! You're burning fat and keeping the pace.
feedback.discipline: Weight is going down, discipline is up. Keep it up!
feedback.protein: Perfect protein. You're protecting your muscle mass.
feedback.mvp: MVP of the week for your gym consistency.
feedback.consistencyKey: Consistency is key. Let's go for next week!

training.recovery: 🧘‍♂️ Recovery Priority. Focus on mobility or a light walk.

habit.hydrationCopy: Drink 500ml of water as soon as you wake up to kickstart your metabolism.

correlation.explanation: Here we look for hidden correlations between your habits. These aren't absolute truths, but patterns emerging from your own data to help you adjust your performance.

points.fuel: Fuel: Does consuming more carbs yesterday improve your workout volume today?
points.recovery: Recovery: How much does high volume training affect your deep sleep that same night?
points.discipline: Discipline: Is there a connection between your sleep quality and your ability to stay within calorie range?

charts.activitySleepSubtitle: Daily Steps vs Sleep Quality (Next Night)

fuelVsPerf.positive: 🔥 It's working! More carbs yesterday lead to longer workouts today.
fuelVsPerf.negative: 🤷‍♂️ No clear impact of yesterday's carbs on today's duration.
fuelVsPerf.neutral: 🧪 Keep tracking to see if carbs fuel your sessions.

recoveryCost.positive: 💤 Effort pays off: intense workouts are boosting your deep sleep.
recoveryCost.negative: 🧘‍♂️ Your deep sleep seems independent of workout duration.
recoveryCost.neutral: 🧪 Analyzing how today's volume affects your rest tonight.

willpower.positive: 🧠 Connection found: better sleep leads to better diet control.
willpower.negative: 🍕 Your calorie adherence doesn't seem tied to your sleep.
willpower.neutral: 🧪 Seeking if Oura's rest protects your willpower.

metabolic.positive: 📈 We've detected that with higher calorie intake, your weight tends to rise. The engine is well calibrated.
metabolic.negative: 📉 Unusual: your weight drops when you eat more. There might be tracking errors or a 'whoosh effect'.
metabolic.neutral: 🧪 Analyzing how your weekly calories impact real weight change.

activitySleep.positive: 🏃‍♂️ More steps, better sleep! Staying active is optimizing your rest architecture.
activitySleep.negative: 🥱 Too much activity seems to be exhausting you and lowering your sleep quality.
activitySleep.neutral: 🧪 Seeking your ideal activity level for the deepest rest.

hydrationCardio.positive: 💧 Hydration confirmed: water is helping raise your HRV and lower stress.
hydrationCardio.negative: 🥛 Your HRV doesn't seem to react directly to water. Review your stress sources.
hydrationCardio.neutral: 🧪 Analyzing the impact of water on your cardiovascular recovery.

correlation.footer: Patterns become clearer as you log more days. Keep tracking to unlock real insights.

iosShortcut.title: iOS Shortcut
iosShortcut.subtitle: Log weight and steps from your iPhone

iosShortcut.copyIdHint: Copy your ID before installing. The shortcut will ask for it during setup.

appleHealth.title: Apple Health
appleHealth.subtitle: Sync steps, weight, workouts and sleep from your iPhone
appleHealth.noSync: No sync has been received from Apple Health yet.
appleHealth.tokenError: The sync token could not be generated. Sign in again and retry.
appleHealth.howTo: How to create the iOS Shortcut
appleHealth.dedupeNote: Manual and Oura entries always win: the sync never overwrites an existing entry for the day. Imported workouts are tagged with [apple_health].
steps: Open the Shortcuts app on your iPhone and create a new shortcut.
Add "Find Health Samples" actions: today's steps (sum), latest weight, and optionally today's sleep and workouts.
Add a "Get Contents of URL" action with the endpoint URL above.
Set it to POST with a JSON body, shaped like the example below, pasting your sync token and the Health variables.
Run the shortcut once: the response should say "success": true.
In the Automation tab, create a Personal Automation → Time of Day (e.g. 11:30 PM, daily) → Run Shortcut, and turn off "Ask Before Running".
